steps.title: Set up the weight-volume relationship
steps.content:
Total weight equals weight per cubic foot times volume. If $x$ is the total weight and $V$ is the volume in cubic feet:
$$x = 160V$$

steps.title: Multiply the volume inequality by 160
steps.content:
The volume constraint is $12.7 < V < 15.7$. Multiplying all parts by the positive constant 160 preserves the inequality direction:
$$160(12.7) < 160V < 160(15.7)$$
Substitute $x = 160V$:
$$160(12.7) < x < 160(15.7)$$
This matches choice C — C is correct.
